comment_text.sn21.11:1.2: One of the great disciples, said to be foremost in advising monks (AN 1.231), as shown in the next discourse. He is seen meditating at SN 54.7, and his verses are at Thag 10.3.
comment_text.sn21.11:1.5: As a Western monk, I feel seen.

root_text.sn21.11:1.2: Atha kho āyasmā mahākappino yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami.
root_text.sn21.11:1.3: Addasā kho bhagavā āyasmantaṁ mahākappinaṁ dūratova āgacchantaṁ.
root_text.sn21.11:1.5: “passatha no tumhe, bhikkhave, etaṁ bhikkhuṁ āgacchantaṁ odātakaṁ tanukaṁ tuṅganāsikan”ti?
root_text.sn21.11:1.8: Na ca sā samāpatti sulabharūpā yā tena bhikkhunā asamāpannapubbā.
root_text.sn21.11:1.9: Yassa catthāya kulaputtā sammadeva agārasmā anagāriyaṁ pabbajanti tadanuttaraṁ brahmacariyapariyosānaṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja viharatī”ti.
root_text.sn21.11:2.2: Idaṁ vatvāna sugato athāparaṁ etadavoca satthā:

translation_text.sn21.11:1.2: Then Venerable Mahākappina went to see the Buddha.
translation_text.sn21.11:1.3: The Buddha saw him coming off in the distance,
translation_text.sn21.11:1.5: “Mendicants, do you see that monk coming—white, thin, with a pointy nose?”
translation_text.sn21.11:1.7: “That mendicant is very mighty and powerful.
translation_text.sn21.11:1.8: It’s not easy to find an attainment that he has not already attained.
translation_text.sn21.11:1.9: And he has realized the supreme end of the spiritual path in this very life. He lives having achieved with his own insight the goal for which gentlemen rightly go forth from the lay life to homelessness.”
translation_text.sn21.11:2.1: That is what the Buddha said.
translation_text.sn21.11:2.2: Then the Holy One, the Teacher, went on to say:
translation_text.sn21.11:3.2: who take clan as the standard.
translation_text.sn21.11:3.3: But one accomplished in knowledge and conduct
translation_text.sn21.11:3.4: is best among gods and humans.
translation_text.sn21.11:4.4: and the brahmin shines in absorption.
translation_text.sn21.11:4.5: But all day and all night,

variant_text.sn21.11:4.5: Atha sabbamahorattiṁ → atha sabbamahorattaṁ (bj, sya-all, km)
